summary_text: 1) `Start` - Set run mode to **draft-only**. - Confirm flow is **lead-post (Forbesâ†’note style)** and choose `Body` + `Source URLs` as canonical targets. - Stop condition: user later requests immediate publish or schedule without explicit confirmation. 2) `Source extraction` - Pull article title, section-level summary, and 3â€“5 claim-backed points from the URL. - Create a compact evidence list: each claim + source quote/snippet + URL anchor. - Stop condition: page is inaccessible, paywalled, or key claims cannot be traced to the article text. 3) `Drafting` - Draft `Body` in Japanese, then align `Body JA` only if needed. - Keep target length ~`800â€“1000ĺ­—` for schedulable-ready shape. - Avoid direct X/twitter links in the body for this flow; keep text generalized unless user explicitly allows brand-specific wording. - Stop condition: any required claim lacks a corresponding source snippet. 4) `Leakage + policy checks` - Run checks: mode, length target, no forbidden platform links in body, no improper proper nouns/entities in publish-facing text or thumbnail cue text, `Source URLs` populated, required article URL present. - Stop condition: leakage check fails or policy violations remain. 5) `Pre-flight handoff` - Return draft + evidence list + status (`ready`, `blocked`) in a compact format. - If blocked, stop and ask only for specific missing approval (e.g., brand name allowed, schedule switch, or alternate source permission). 6) `Handoff only when clear` - If all checks pass, deliver draft package for sync step. - Do not proceed to approve/schedule/posting from this lane without an explicit user intent switch.
